subject: EMS
title: Supply & Demand
tagline: Watch prices change as supply and demand shift in the market!

stages.name: Stage 1
stages.question: What happens to price when demand increases but supply stays the same?
options: It increases
A product that can replace another, like tea for coffee
It tends to be high due to low supply
Price rises
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! Price rises is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is Price rises.

stages.name: Stage 2
stages.question: What happens to price when supply increases but demand stays the same?
options: The price where supply equals demand
How much of a product is available to sell
Price falls
When demand is greater than supply
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! Price falls is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is Price falls.

stages.name: Stage 3
stages.question: What is 'demand'?
options: How much of a product is available to sell
A product that can replace another, like tea for coffee
How much of a product people want to buy
It increases
stages.correctOption: How much of a product people want to buy
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! How much of a product people want to buy is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is How much of a product people want to buy.

stages.name: Stage 4
stages.question: What is 'supply'?
options: When there isn't enough of something to meet demand
How much of a product is available to sell
It tends to be high due to low supply
The value of the next best option given up
stages.correctOption: How much of a product is available to sell
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! How much of a product is available to sell is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is How much of a product is available to sell.

stages.name: Stage 5
stages.question: What is 'scarcity'?
options: How much of a product people want to buy
It increases
It reduces supply, so prices rise
When there isn't enough of something to meet demand
stages.correctOption: When there isn't enough of something to meet demand
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! When there isn't enough of something to meet demand is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is When there isn't enough of something to meet demand.

stages.name: Stage 6
stages.question: What is 'equilibrium price'?
options: Price falls
The price where supply equals demand
It reduces supply, so prices rise
A product that can replace another, like tea for coffee
stages.correctOption: The price where supply equals demand
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! The price where supply equals demand is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is The price where supply equals demand.

stages.name: Stage 7
stages.question: What happens to demand for umbrellas on a rainy day?
options: How much of a product is available to sell
It increases
It tends to keep prices lower
The price where supply equals demand
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! It increases is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is It increases.

stages.name: Stage 8
stages.question: What is a 'surplus'?
options: When supply is greater than demand
It reduces supply, so prices rise
Price rises
Price falls
stages.correctOption: When supply is greater than demand
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! When supply is greater than demand is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is When supply is greater than demand.

stages.name: Stage 9
stages.question: What is a 'shortage'?
options: It reduces supply, so prices rise
The price where supply equals demand
When supply is greater than demand
When demand is greater than supply
stages.correctOption: When demand is greater than supply
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! When demand is greater than supply is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is When demand is greater than supply.

stages.name: Stage 10
stages.question: Why do prices of school uniforms rise in January?
options: When supply is greater than demand
When there isn't enough of something to meet demand
The price where supply equals demand
High demand as schools reopen
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! High demand as schools reopen is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is High demand as schools reopen.

stages.name: Stage 11
stages.question: What is 'opportunity cost'?
options: The value of the next best option given up
It reduces supply, so prices rise
Price rises
Price falls
stages.correctOption: The value of the next best option given up
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! The value of the next best option given up is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is The value of the next best option given up.

stages.name: Stage 12
stages.question: How does competition affect prices?
options: Price falls
It tends to keep prices lower
It tends to be high due to low supply
It increases
stages.correctOption: It tends to keep prices lower
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! It tends to keep prices lower is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is It tends to keep prices lower.

stages.name: Stage 13
stages.question: What happens to the price of a rare collectible item?
options: A product that can replace another, like tea for coffee
When demand is greater than supply
It reduces supply, so prices rise
It tends to be high due to low supply
stages.correctOption: It tends to be high due to low supply
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! It tends to be high due to low supply is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is It tends to be high due to low supply.

stages.name: Stage 14
stages.question: What is a 'substitute good'?
options: When demand is greater than supply
A product that can replace another, like tea for coffee
Price falls
It tends to be high due to low supply
stages.correctOption: A product that can replace another, like tea for coffee
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! A product that can replace another, like tea for coffee is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is A product that can replace another, like tea for coffee.

stages.name: Stage 15
stages.question: How can a drought affect the price of maize?
options: It reduces supply, so prices rise
The value of the next best option given up
A product that can replace another, like tea for coffee
When demand is greater than supply
stages.correctOption: It reduces supply, so prices rise
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! It reduces supply, so prices rise is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is It reduces supply, so prices rise.
